summary: Joel Spolsky briefly recommends a resource for setting executive compensation in tech companies. His friend Noam at Harvard Business School conducts annual surveys of executive compensation. The surveys help founders benchmark pay and equity for executive roles. The data can be purchased for $999 or accessed free by participating in the survey.
key_points: Annual executive compensation surveys from Harvard Business School provide benchmarking data for tech companies
The surveys help answer practical questions like VP of Sales salary or whether a CTO equity grant is fair
Participating in the survey grants free access to results, creating an incentive for broad participation
money_quotes: The surveys are a great way to figure out how much to pay that VP of Sales you were thinking of hiring, or whether your equity grant as CTO is fair.
key_insight: Compensation benchmarking surveys are a valuable tool for tech founders making executive hiring and pay decisions.
